messages.content:
Match each artist with the appropriate genre.
Group of answer choices
Rush

American Punk
Billy Joel

British Punk
The Clash

Progressive Rock
The Ramones

Singer-Songwiriter
The Police

American New Wave
The Cars

British New Wave

Rush - Progressive Rock
Billy Joel - Singer-Songwriter
The Clash - British Punk
The Ramones - American Punk
The Police - American New Wave
The Cars - British New Wave

“Turn on, tune in, and drop out” was the slogan of which proponent of LSD?
Group of answer choices

Aldous Huxley

Brian Wilson

Paul Kantner

Timothy Leary

Both the Beach Boys and the Beatles were on which U.S. record label?
Group of answer choices

Apple

Capitol

AOR

EMI

Musically, “White Rabbit” by Jefferson Airplane is based on:
Group of answer choices

avant-garde classical music

country folk songs

modal jazz improvisation

a Spanish bolero

Which member of Pink Floyd was replaced by Dave Gilmour?
Group of answer choices

Ginger Baker

Richard Wright

Syd Barrett

Roger Waters

Ozzy Osbourne sang lead vocals for which British band?
Group of answer choices

The Faces

Jethro Tull

Black Sabbath

Deep Purple

The original lead singer of Genesis was:
Group of answer choices

Phil Collins

Peter Gabriel

Michael Rutherford

Syd Barrett

Which musician helped popularize reggae outside of Jamaica with a cover of “I Shot the Sheriff”?
Group of answer choices

David Bowie

Eric Clapton

Peter Tosh

Jimi Hendrix

Rush is an example of:
Group of answer choices

a progressive rock–influenced band

new wave

a blues-influenced rock band

late ’70s corporate rock
